channel.title: Redds on Stephen D. Gregory
channel.description: Recent content in Redds on Stephen D. Gregory

item.title: Calculate distances along a river in R and GRASS
item.description: Recently, I have been helping https://www.gwct.org.uk/research/staff/fisheries/jmarsh/ to develop a method of calculating distances along linear features. Specifically, we were measuring the distances between salmonid redd locations and juvenile survey locations on the river Frome, Dorset, UK. Together, we devise a generalisable method to calculate the least cost distance between points located on a complex river network. Our method includes the following steps: convert the river polygon dataset to a high resolution raster; thin that high resolution raster to represent the river as a network of single pixel width linear features; shift the sample site coordinates to the closest river pixel in the thinned raster; and calculate the least cost distance between a set of coordinates.

item.title: New paper: Flow and Atlantic salmon redd distribution
item.description: Our paper “The effects of flow on Atlantic salmon (Salmo salar) redd distribution in a UK chalk stream between 1980 and 2015” is out today in Ecology of Freshwater Fish! Parry ES, Gregory SD, Lauridsen RB, and Griffiths SW. The effects of flow on Atlantic salmon (Salmo salar) redd distribution in a UK chalk stream between 1980 and 2015. Ecol Freshw Fish. 2017;00:1–10. doi:[10.1111/eff.12330](http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/eff.12330/abstract) Abstract Atlantic salmon are an ecologically and economically important migratory fish in the UK, whose stocks have been declining over the past 30 years.
